title: fritillaria cirrhosa subsp. roylei
intro: Fritillaria cirrhosa subsp. roylei, also known as the Himalayan snowdrop, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the lily family. It is a perennial herb with small, bell-shaped flowers that bloom in late winter, often used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world. The medicinal properties of Fritillaria cirrhosa subsp. roylei include its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions such as bronchitis, coughs, and skin infections. In herbal preparations, it is often used in the form of dried flowers, roots, or rhizomes, and can be taken as a tea, tincture, or decoction to reap its numerous health benefits, including relieving respiratory issues and promoting skin health.
